description:
You are a backup-verification engineer who has just received the file
    “/home/user/source_sales.db”, a small SQLite-3 database that contains a single
    table named “sales”.  Your goal is to:

    1. Create an exact migration/backup of the database under the name
       “/home/user/target_sales.db”.
    2. Validate that the number of rows in table “sales” is **identical**
       in the source and the migrated (target) database.
    3. Write the validation results to the file
       “/home/user/migration_validation.log” in the **exact** format specified
       below.

    The entire activity must be completed with **one** compound terminal
    command (shell operators such as “&&”, “;”, pipes, or subshells are
    allowed, but it must be entered as a single line).

    Required log-file format (every line terminated by a single newline):
        SOURCE_ROW_COUNT=<integer>
        TARGET_ROW_COUNT=<integer>
        STATUS=OK

    Notes & constraints:
    • “STATUS” must be the literal string “OK” only if the counts match;
      otherwise it must be “MISMATCH”.
    • The log file path **must** be “/home/user/migration_validation.log”.
    • Overwrite the log file if it already exists.
    • Do NOT display any row data—only the row counts.
    • You may use any standard Linux, core-utils, or SQLite-3 CLI features.
    • Remember: the entire job (copy + validation + log write) should be
      executed through a single terminal command entered at the prompt.
truth:
Initial system state:
    • File “/home/user/source_sales.db” already exists and is a valid SQLite-3
      database containing:
        Table: sales
            Columns: id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, item TEXT, qty INTEGER
            Row count: 5
    • No file named “/home/user/target_sales.db” exists at start-up.
    • No file named “/home/user/migration_validation.log” exists at start-up.

    Expected final state AFTER the agent finishes correctly:
    1. File “/home/user/target_sales.db” exists and is byte-for-byte identical
       to “/home/user/source_sales.db”.
    2. File “/home/user/migration_validation.log” exists with **exactly** the
       following three lines and nothing more:
        SOURCE_ROW_COUNT=5\n
        TARGET_ROW_COUNT=5\n
        STATUS=OK\n

    The automated grader will:
        • Compute SHA-256 of both databases and confirm equality.
        • Read “/home/user/migration_validation.log” and verify the three-line
          format and the exact numeric values (5 and 5) followed by STATUS=OK.
